_doc: Stable named-entity registry for the Maia Founder OS persona. ALL ENTITIES FICTIONAL. Every named entity in every snapshot file MUST trace back here for cross-snapshot consistency. Adversarial pattern-matching against this list against real public records (LinkedIn, GitHub, USPTO, Crunchbase) MUST produce zero real-Shruti hits.

_relationship_to_maia_DO_NOT_DEVIATE.origin: Sibling-of-college-roommate connection (Maia's college roommate's older sibling). Met ~2017 through that sibling. Worked together briefly on a hackathon team that placed 3rd at a major academic-conference student competition. Stayed in touch. Started talking about a joint venture in late 2025 when Maia's day-job got constraint-bound.
_relationship_to_maia_DO_NOT_DEVIATE.relationship_status: Friends + co-founders. NOT romantic. NOT married. NOT dating. NEVER were.
_relationship_to_maia_DO_NOT_DEVIATE.reason_for_constraint: Founder-couple framing is the most personally-identifying shape Shruti has. It does NOT get reused in any persona. Adversarial pattern-matching against any persona must NOT recover the founder-couple shape.
_relationship_to_maia_DO_NOT_DEVIATE.communication_register: Asynchronous Slack + occasional in-person (Devon Bay-Area-based, Maia Austin-based). Co-founder dailies are video calls, not in-person.

company.name: Veridian
company.tagline_primary: Operational knowledge layer for solo operators running agentic teams.
company.tagline_alt_speedrun_register: An inner workspace for solo operators running agentic teams.
company.tagline_alt_yc_register: Company Brain for the founder before the company exists.
company._tagline_strategy: Three taglines, all consistent. Primary tagline lands as a 'Company Brain'-adjacent claim (YC #4 thesis register: 'extracts and structures fragmented operational knowledge'). Speedrun-register variant is the warmer founder-OS framing the SR007 demo uses. YC-register variant lands explicitly within YC's stated rfs language. Maia swaps among the three depending on which audience the demo is constructed for.

thesis_alignment.yc_rfs_company_brain_4: Veridian extracts and structures fragmented operational knowledge from a solo founder's email + meeting transcripts + code repos + drafts. Not search, not chatbots — typed event log + bounded multi-agent reads + correction stream. Same architectural pattern YC's Tom Blomfield describes for Company Brain at company scale, applied to the individual operator scale.
thesis_alignment.yc_rfs_ai_os_for_companies_15: Veridian is the AI Operating System claim Diana Hu describes — but for the founder before the company exists. Single intelligence layer reasoning across all the operator's context. Not another dashboard — infrastructure for individual-scale legibility.
thesis_alignment.speedrun_sr007: Veridian is on-thesis for Speedrun's editorial frame ('AI does the work, you guarantee the outcome') — bounded multi-agent diligence does the work; the correction stream guarantees the outcome sharpens over time. Founder-first not category-first per Speedrun's stated evaluation rubric.

company.product_surface: Local-first desktop app. Drag-window-into-tray ingest. Bounded-multi-agent disagreement on ingested context. Correction stream as the moat.
company.architectural_pattern: Same architectural pattern as Liminal — the persona demonstrates Liminal's mechanics through a synthetic surface. Cross-thesis-aligned: YC #4 + YC #15 + Speedrun SR007 all hit at the architectural layer.

lead_artifact.name: Founder Day Trust Packet
lead_artifact.subject: End-of-day fundraise-decision retrospective with corrections, disagreements, and audit chain
lead_artifact.buyer_pay_for: $49 per packet, or unlimited via Founder OS subscription
lead_artifact.packet_shape: header (eyebrow + title + disposition) + subject (the single decision) + bounded reads (12 agents) + refusals (with named-route to right agent) + disagreements (with evidence anchors) + corrections (9-tag taxonomy) + disposition (approve / escalate / route / hold / refuse) + audit chain (SHA-256 + provenance + vault commit ID) + outcome strip (open-loop → closed-loop → artifact)
lead_artifact._packet_abstraction_reference: Per `liminal-ip/06-evidence/INVENTIONS.md` §10 — the packet is the bridge between Liminal's substrate (correction stream + 12 bounded agents + vault) and Liminal's deliverable (what the user pays for). The substrate is the moat; the packet is the deliverable. Each persona ships its own packet name + content with the same architectural shape.
shipped_packet_siblings_in_corpus: Mission Trust Packet (Connor Walsh / Defense / Battalion S6)
Board Decision Packet (Maia / Founder OS / fundraise close — this persona)
AgentActionPacket (Darius Okonkwo-class / Enterprise / Head of AI Product)
Pilot Decision Packet (Darius Okonkwo / Business / VP Support)
Sprint Correction Packet (Lena Park / Team / VP Eng)

hackathon.name: AgentScale Q2 Showcase
hackathon.shape: Pattern-faithful to AgentHansa: judging cohort, judge-feedback timing pattern, public marketplace deploy-flow
hackathon.judge_feedback_verbatim: Veridian's bounded-specialist refusal pattern was the most architecturally distinct entry in our cohort. Most agents in the cohort hallucinated capability when given out-of-domain prompts; yours refused with named-route. That's a thoughtful answer to the AI-overreach problem and the local-first vault posture is a credible audit/compliance signal.
hackathon._strategic_substrate: the synthetic 'judge-feedback as proof-point' beat the demo uses, parallel to real AgentHansa quote

co_authored_paper.title: Argus — Distributed Systems Testing under Adversarial Latency Conditions
co_authored_paper.venue: TBD (distributed-systems venue: NSDI / OSDI / SOSP / EuroSys / SIGMOD)
co_authored_paper.submission_target: forward in time; venue not yet visible from the artifact itself
co_authored_paper.architectural_relevance_to_veridian: Argus orchestrates parallel test-evaluators with bounded specialization (timing analyzer, consistency analyzer, partition-tolerance analyzer) — same multi-evaluator orchestration pattern Veridian uses in its agent runtime, applied to distributed-systems testing.
co_authored_paper._why_distinct_from_heron: Heron is security research benchmarking against CodeQL; Argus is distributed-systems research benchmarking against Jepsen. Same architectural-claim shape, different research domain, different baseline tool. Adversarial pattern-matching does NOT recover Heron from Argus.

_critical_DO_NOTS: Do NOT introduce founder-couple framing into Maia/Devon — they are friends + cofounders only.
Do NOT use any real venture name (a16z Speedrun, YC, AgentHansa, Sequoia, etc.) in any snapshot.

Do NOT use any real partner name (Andrew Chen, Ryan Rigney, Tom Hammer, Fareed Mosavat, Peter Dimov, Marcus Segal, Sam Shank, Kenan Saleh, Emily Bennett, Jon Lai, Daniela Park ≠ real Daniela, James Okafor ≠ real James, etc.) in any snapshot.
Do NOT use any real company name where Shruti or Shayaun worked (Asana, Cloudflare, Robinhood, Ancestry, Yelp, Twitch, Voxel, Rubrik) in any snapshot.
Do NOT use 'Heron' or 'liminal-agents' or any real Liminal artifact name. Use Argus and Veridian respectively.
Do NOT use 'MENA Group' or 'Haas' or 'AMENA Center' or 'YC finalist at 21' anywhere.
Do NOT echo the real Liminal one-line pitch ('Liminal gives form to inner life'). Use Veridian's synthetic tagline only.
Do NOT include any real .eml header chain. All synthetic .eml files generate fresh RFC-822 headers.
Do NOT include real timestamps that match Shruti's known calendar — synthetic timestamps offset by ~1 hour from real to break temporal correlation.
